contract.name: v2/autocomplete/accounts/main.md
contract.description: Autocomplete search for Treasury main account codes using Treasury Account Symbol component filters.

filters.description: Treasury account component filters controlling which main account codes are returned.
filters.constraints: Optional. Recognized keys: ata, aid, bpoa, epoa, a, main, sub. Each accepts either null or a non-empty string; leading and trailing whitespace is trimmed before matching. 'main' performs prefix matching; other keys require exact matches. Empty strings raise 422 validation errors and non-string types raise 400 errors. If 'filters' is omitted, null, or any non-object value, the server ignores it and returns unfiltered results. Unknown keys are ignored without error.

limit.description: Maximum number of autocomplete suggestions to return.
limit.constraints: Optional with default 10. Must be an integer between 0 and 500 inclusive. Numeric strings (including whitespace-padded values) are coerced to integers. Values above 500 return a 422 error. Zero returns an empty list. Negative integers trigger an HTML 500. Floats, booleans, null, arrays, and other non-integer types return 400 validation errors.

results.description: Array of main account code strings sorted ascending. Empty array when no matches.

quirks: 'filters' can be omitted or provided as null, scalars, arrays, or strings; the server ignores malformed values and returns unfiltered results.
Component filter values are trimmed for whitespace, and unknown component keys are silently ignored, so typos often result in unfiltered or empty responses without errors.
'limit' accepts numeric strings (including with leading whitespace) but still enforces a hard ceiling of 500; negatives surface as HTML 500 errors instead of JSON.
risks: Negative 'limit' values trigger HTML 500 responses, so callers must validate limits client-side to avoid unexpected error formats.
Silently ignored or trimmed component keys make it easy to ship queries that return unfiltered or empty result sets without signaling a problem.
Large limits up to 500 return sizable payloads and repeated probing showed occasional dropped connections, so callers should implement retries.
gaps: Effectiveness of null filters for components other than 'a' remains unclear; observed responses were either empty or indistinguishable from baseline results.
Response ordering appears ascending but the service returns no metadata confirming ordering or total counts.

probes.notes: Prefix search returns codes starting with 30.
Explicit empty filters object returns default top 10.
Null body is interpreted as no filters.
Leading whitespace in component values is trimmed before filtering.
Empty strings for component filters raise 422 validation errors.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Invalid value in 'filters|main'. '30' is not a valid type (text)"}

probes.notes: Non-string component values fail with 400 type errors.
Whitespace-padded numeric strings are coerced for limit validation.

mismatches: Docs mark 'filters' as required object, but the API accepts omitted or non-object filters and still returns results.
Docs only describe 'limit' as optional number; live API enforces a 0-500 integer range with a hard 422 above the ceiling and 500 errors for negatives.
gaps: Effectiveness of null filters for components other than 'a' remains unclear; observed responses were either empty or indistinguishable from baseline results.
Response ordering appears ascending but the service returns no metadata confirming ordering or total counts.
risks: Negative 'limit' values trigger HTML 500 responses, so callers must validate limits client-side to avoid unexpected error formats.
Silently ignored or trimmed component keys make it easy to ship queries that return unfiltered or empty result sets without signaling a problem.
Large limits up to 500 return sizable payloads and repeated probing showed occasional dropped connections, so callers should implement retries.
